title: Magnetic Quantum Criticality inside the Superconducting State Revealed by Penetration Depth Scaling with Local $T_{\mathrm c}$
authors: Yusuke Iguchi, Kaede Inoh, Ryosuke Koizumi, Makoto Yokoyama
abstract: We demonstrate a magnetic quantum critical point embedded within the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn$_5$, revealed by a pronounced peak in the magnetic penetration depth at zero temperature $λ(0)$. Using scanning SQUID microscopy, we determine the local superconducting transition temperature $T_{\mathrm c}$ and $λ(0)$. By parameterizing $λ(0)$ in terms of the local $T_{\mathrm c}$ rather than nominal Zn substitution, we circumvent the ambiguity caused by doping inhomogeneity and enable a more precise extraction of the critical exponent. The extracted exponent exceeds the clean spin-density-wave value, indicating a disorder-modified quantum critical regime. The enhancement of $λ(0)$ reflects the suppression of the superfluid stiffness and is consistent with critical scaling. Our approach provides a route to uncover intrinsic quantum critical behavior hidden by inhomogeneity in unconventional superconductors.
summary: Researchers used scanning SQUID microscopy to reveal a magnetic quantum critical point hidden inside the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn5. By using local transition temperatures to account for doping disorder, they identified a disorder-modified quantum critical regime. This method provides a new way to study intrinsic quantum criticality in inhomogeneous unconventional superconductors.
main_problem: Identifying a magnetic quantum critical point hidden within the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn5, which is typically obscured by doping-induced inhomogeneity.
main_result: The discovery of a magnetic quantum critical point embedded in the superconducting state, characterized by a disorder-modified critical exponent that exceeds the clean spin-density-wave value.
method: Scanning SQUID microscopy was used to measure local superconducting transition temperature (Tc) and magnetic penetration depth (lambda(0)), allowing for scaling analysis based on local Tc to bypass doping inhomogeneity.
key_observables: Local superconducting transition temperature (Tc) and magnetic penetration depth at zero temperature (lambda(0)).
important_parameters: Zn substitution level, local Tc, and the critical exponent of the magnetic penetration depth.
main_assumptions: The scaling of lambda(0) can be accurately parameterized by local Tc rather than nominal Zn concentration to account for disorder.
paper_structure: The paper presents the experimental observation of a peak in lambda(0), describes the scanning SQUID methodology for local measurements, performs scaling analysis using local Tc, and compares the extracted critical exponent to the clean spin-density-wave limit.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
